about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with something called the American Jazz Orchestra, with John Lewis... And this was a band comprised of mostly big band veterans: Marvin Stamm, Virgil Jones...
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: I would have to say in the studios that was the most adept ensemble I have ever played with... Let Eddie Daniels play, let me play, let Virgil Jones play a little bit
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I had Martin Banks, Virgil Jones, and Johnny Coles on trumpets.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Ridley
about_quotes.evidence: I was a part of a music club and all the young musicians around town like Freddie Hubbard, Virgil Jones, Mel Ryan and David Hardiman, on and on, we all came up playing jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: So you had Virgil Jones... so you brought in- The older guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Well, Virgil Jones is in there. ... I had an article written in the New York Times Sunday section, many years ago called “Jazz in the Middle Ages,” where I talked about the fact that the musicians in their middle ages... are getting no play whatsoever... about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
